prose:
Method–provenance block
• Property: atom-condensed Fukui indices (Mulliken charges) for furan; headline value reported is the largest electrophilic (f⁺) index.  
• Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier ‘standard’), exact four-center integrals (no density fitting) in PySCF.  
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz (9 atoms), single-point N, N ± 1 charge-state calculations; no further optimisation (DFT//input geometry).  
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).  
• Charge / multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation (+1, doublet), anion (–1, doublet).  
• Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (engine subcommand `fukui`; PySCF backend).  
• Convergence & caveats: all three SCF calculations converged; integrity status = ok, trustworthy = true.  Warnings reproduced verbatim below.

Result
Most electrophilic site: C5 (atom index 4) with f⁺ = 1.9242931 (dimensionless).  
Most nucleophilic site: C5 (atom index 4) with f⁻ = 0.1616371.
